title: Multiplying a Whole Number by a Fraction: 3 × 2/5

steps.explanation: Multiplying by a whole number is the same as repeated addition of the fraction.

steps.subgoalLabel: Multiply numerator by the whole number
steps.instruction: Multiply the whole number (3) by the numerator (2). Keep the denominator.
steps.explanation: Only the numerator changes because we are taking more pieces of the same size.

steps.subgoalLabel: Convert to a mixed number if needed
steps.instruction: Is 6/5 an improper fraction? If so, convert it.
steps.explanation: Since the numerator is larger than the denominator, we can write it as a mixed number.

title: Multiplying a Fraction by a Whole Number: 4 × 3/4

steps.instruction: Multiply the whole number by the numerator.
steps.explanation: We are taking 4 groups of 3 fourths, which gives us 12 fourths.
12 fourths equals exactly 3 wholes. When the numerator is divisible by the denominator, the result is a whole number.
Four groups of three-fourths makes exactly 3 wholes.
